tldr: Headlines split into two wholly incompatible New York stories: international outlets (France, India, Russia, Singapore) foreground the Times Square shooting in their titles, making the day a crime scene, while AFP, CBC, and The New Yorker frame the Knicks win as a cultural watershed. US conservative outlets bypass both stories to attack Mayor Zohran Mamdani by name in their headlines. VnExpress's 'smashed vehicles, littered streets' title is the cluster's most sensationalized framing — treating fan celebration as near-riot before the shooting occurred. The UK Independent's capacity-overflow angle — fans turned away, viewing areas full — fits no active frame, placing it in silences.

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline to use the word 'panic' as the operative noun — Russian state media converts the Times Square gunshot into an American-disorder narrative, foregrounding emotional contagion rather than the near-zero injury count.

Opens with 'Smashed vehicles, littered streets' and 'chaotic celebration' — the most sensationalized title in the cluster, treating fan euphoria as near-riot hours before the Times Square shooting occurred.
'Transforms the City' — sole headline to frame the championship win as an urban identity event rather than a sports result, a police incident, or a crime scene.
Only headline focused on crowd-logistics failure: 'viewing areas hit capacity early in the morning' — an operational angle absent from every other outlet, landing the UK bucket in silences.
'World Cup? Not today' is the cluster's only headline to contextualize the Knicks parade against a competing mega-event, framing basketball fandom as displacing global football coverage in New York.

bottom_line: The headline layer presents three wholly separate New York stories depending on outlet: a crime scene (France, India, Russia, Singapore foregrounding the Times Square shooting), a civic and cultural triumph (AFP, CBC, The New Yorker), and a political liability for Mayor Mamdani (US conservative media) — three frames with zero overlap, illustrating the sensationalism-divergence the divergence index is built to measure.
